name: build.ts
description: Core build calculation engine that accumulates equipment stats, perks, CDR, boosts, and crit to produce a final BuildResult from a BuildState.
responsibilities: Calculate cooldown reduction (CDR) for runes and weapon arts from perks, race modifiers, and toggles
Calculate damage boosts from perks, race effects, and contextual conditions
Accumulate stats and perks from all equipment slots (armor, ring, rune, weapon, infusion)
Apply enchantments, upgrades, and shrine multipliers to each slot
Handle Monk guild weapon (glove+essence) vs standard weapon (blade+handle) paths
Finalize perks with Perk Effectiveness and Cursed multipliers
Compute crit, buffs, and assemble the complete BuildResult

name: data.ts
description: Barrel re-export module that aggregates all game data accessors from the data/ subdirectory into a single import point.
responsibilities: Re-export character data (races, guilds, perks)
Re-export equipment data (armors, rings, runes, enchantments)
Re-export weapon data (blades, handles, gloves, essences)

name: character.ts
description: Loads race, guild, and perk data from JSON files and provides lookup functions for accessing individual entries by name.
responsibilities: Import and type-cast race, guild, and perk JSON data
Build name-to-object lookup maps for O(1) access
Provide getRace, getGuild, getPerk, getGuildRank accessor functions
Provide isMonkGuild helper

name: equipment.ts
description: Loads armor, ring, rune, and enchantment data from JSON files and provides lookup/accessor functions for equipment items and their parts.
responsibilities: Import and type-cast armor, ring, rune, and enchantment JSON data
Build armor map merging shared part data with per-part overrides
Provide getArmorPart, getEnchant, getRing, getRune accessor functions
Provide armorSupportsSlot and getArmorSlotTypes utilities
Re-export raw enchantments array for enchant.ts perk name extraction

name: weapons.ts
description: Loads weapon blade, handle, monk glove, and monk essence data from JSON files and provides lookup functions for each weapon part.
responsibilities: Import and type-cast blade, handle, glove, and essence JSON data
Build name-to-object lookup maps for each weapon part type
Provide getBlade, getHandle, getGlove, getEssence accessor functions

name: dmgTypeBonuses.ts
description: Defines perk-based damage type bonus definitions and a builder function that computes per-element bonus multipliers from active perks and context.
responsibilities: Define PerkDmgTypeBonusDef entries for Void Rage (hex), Channeled Weapon (magic), Emotional (fire), and Draconic Blood
Compute conditional per-type bonus multipliers based on perk stacks and context (rage, emotional state, draconic infusion)
Build a complete damage type bonus map via buildDmgTypeBonuses

name: enchant.ts
description: Handles enchantment application logic including stat modifier application, perk effectiveness scaling, infusion halving, and enchant slot exclusivity enforcement.
responsibilities: Apply enchantment stat modifiers to equipment slots in two phases (selectors then direct modifiers)
Apply upgrade levels to modified base stats while preserving armor penetration
Apply enchantment perk modifiers and effects to perk map
Compute Perk Effectiveness and Cursed multipliers with exempt perk tracking
Apply infusion 0.5x stat scaling
Enforce exclusive enchant slot rules and deduplication

name: engine.ts
description: Top-level barrel re-export that aggregates the entire engine module (data, shrine, enchant, weapon, build, format) into a single import.
responsibilities: Re-export all engine sub-modules as a unified public API

name: format.ts
description: Provides utility functions for formatting stat values (with +/- signs and % suffix for percent stats) and converting camelCase stat keys to human-readable labels.
responsibilities: Format numeric stat values with sign prefix and optional percent suffix
Convert camelCase stat key names to spaced title-case labels

name: shrine.ts
description: Implements shrine tier multiplier logic that scales weapon stats and scalings based on item tier (Tier 1 gets 3x, Tier 5 gets 1x).
responsibilities: Define tier-to-multiplier mapping (Tier 1: 3.0x down to Tier 5: 1.0x)
Apply shrine multipliers to stat maps
Apply shrine multipliers to scaling maps

name: weapon.ts
description: Calculates complete weapon results by combining blade+handle or glove+essence parts, including damage types, scalings, hybrid detection, weapon type resolution, and monk rank bonuses.
responsibilities: Extract damage types, scalings, and stats from weapon parts
Merge perks and attack speed from both parts
Detect hybrid weapons (different scaling types on each part) for 1.5x scaling bonus
Apply shrine multipliers to scalings and stats
Resolve weapon type from handle+blade combination with perk-based overrides
Handle monk weapon path with rank-based stat bonuses
Build comprehensive WeaponResult with all part data

name: Modal.svelte
description: Reusable base modal component providing overlay backdrop, animated dialog box, focus trapping, close button, and a library of global CSS classes for modal list items, stat pills, perk tags, rank buttons, and filters.
responsibilities: Render modal overlay with fade transition and backdrop blur
Render modal box with fly-in animation and scroll containment
Implement keyboard focus trapping within the modal dialog
Provide global CSS classes (modal-list, modal-item, modal-stat-pill, modal-perk-tag, rank-btn, modal-filters, etc.)
Auto-focus first focusable element on open

name: AccessorySelectModal.svelte
description: Modal for selecting armor (helmet/chestplate/leggings), ring, rune, or infusion slot items. Displays item stats, perks, descriptions, and computes effective/brawny boost values for sorting.
responsibilities: Render item selection list with search, tag filtering, and sort-by-effectiveness
Determine slot type (armor/ring/rune/infusion) and map to correct store key
Compute effective boost and brawny boost values for weapon-scaling-aware sorting
Display item stats, perks, descriptions, and cooldown badges
Update build store on item selection

name: GuildModal.svelte
description: Modal for selecting a guild and its rank. Displays guild names with rank buttons, rank-specific stats, and perks. Supports search filtering and tag-based filtering.
responsibilities: Render guild list with rank selection buttons
Display rank-specific stats and perks for the selected rank
Support search filtering by guild name and perk names
Support tag-based filtering via parent-managed selectedTags
Update build store via setGuild on selection

name: index.ts
description: Barrel re-export that exports all modal components (RaceModal, GuildModal, AccessorySelectModal, WeaponPartModal) from the modals directory.
responsibilities: Re-export all modal Svelte components as named exports

name: RaceModal.svelte
description: Modal for selecting a character race. Displays race names, passive descriptions, and stat modifier pills. Supports search filtering and did-you-mean suggestions.
responsibilities: Render race list with search filtering
Display race passive text and stat modifier pills
Update build store race on selection
Support search and did-you-mean suggestion integration

name: WeaponPartModal.svelte
description: Modal for selecting weapon parts (blade, handle, glove, or essence). Displays tier badges, type badges, attack speed, stats, and perks with tier and type filter dropdowns.
responsibilities: Render weapon part list with tier and type filter dropdowns
Display tier badges, type labels, attack speed, stats via WeaponStatsDisplay, and perks
Support weapon stat filter (include/exclude) for stat-based sorting
Update build store on weapon part selection
Support tag filtering and search with did-you-mean suggestions

name: StatFilterShared.svelte
description: Reusable stat filter chip component with tri-state toggle (include/exclude/none) and a module-level buildStatMaps helper that converts grouped stat definitions into label and color lookup maps.
responsibilities: Render a filter chip button with include/exclude visual states
Provide buildStatMaps static utility to create labelMap and colorMap from stat group definitions
Dispatch click, contextmenu, and keydown events for parent handling
Support dimmed/disabled state and optional prefix label

name: elementalBoosts.ts
description: Defines the canonical list of elemental/stat boost stat definitions and their negative counterparts used for stat filter UI.
responsibilities: Define ELEMENTAL_BOOST_STATS array with key and label for each boost type
Define NEGATIVE_ELEMENTAL_BOOST_STATS with neg: prefixed keys for negative filter state

name: filterActions.ts
description: Provides a factory function that creates toggle/remove/clear actions for managing a tri-state filter map (include/exclude/none) with reactive state callbacks.
responsibilities: Create toggle action that cycles through none -> include -> exclude -> none
Create remove action to delete a filter key
Create clear action to reset all filters
Create keyboard handler for Delete/Shift+Backspace to remove individual filters

name: store.ts
description: Central Svelte store managing the BuildState with localStorage persistence, derived build results, and helper functions for enchantment/armor/ring/guild state mutations.
responsibilities: Define DEFAULT_BUILD with all initial build state values
Implement localStorage load/save with debounce for build persistence
Create writable build store and derived result store via calcBuild
Provide setEnchantment helper with slot enforcement
Provide swapEnchantments for dragging enchantments between slots
Provide setGuild helper with Draconic guild race auto-selection
Provide moveArmorSlot for armor drag-and-drop reordering with slot compatibility checks
Provide swapRingWithInfusion for ring/infusion swapping
Provide clearBuild to reset to defaults

name: toast.ts
description: Toast notification store that manages a list of transient notifications with add/remove actions and auto-dismiss by duration.
responsibilities: Maintain writable toast list store
Provide addToast function with auto-generated IDs and configurable duration
Provide removeToast function for manual dismissal
Auto-remove toasts after their duration expires

name: Toast.svelte
description: Toast notification container component that renders a stack of animated toast messages with type-specific styling, icons, and dismiss buttons.
responsibilities: Render fixed-position toast container in top-right corner
Display toast messages with fly-in transition animation
Apply type-specific styles (success/error/info/warning) and icons
Provide dismiss button for each toast
Subscribe to toasts store for reactive updates

name: types.ts
description: Central type definitions file defining all core data structures for the application including StatMap, BuildState, equipment types, enchantments, perks, weapon types, boost entries, and related utility functions.
responsibilities: Define StatKey union type and StatMap from STAT_CONFIG
Define PERCENT_STATS set and STAT_KEYS array
Define Race, Guild, GuildRank, Armor, Ring, Rune interfaces
Define Enchantment, StatModifier, EnchantmentEffect interfaces
Define WeaponBlade, WeaponHandle, MonkGlove, MonkEssence types
Define BuildState interface with all build configuration fields
Define BoostEntry, BoostResult, ProcCoefficient types
Provide applyUpgrade function for stat scaling by upgrade level
Provide getProcCoeffValue, canProc utility functions
Define SCALING_TO_BOOST mapping and ONE_HANDED_TYPES set

name: Badge.svelte
description: Reusable badge/pill UI component supporting multiple color variants (green, amber, red, purple, blue, teal, muted, draco), sizes (xs, sm, md), and shape options (square, solid, mono, tag).
responsibilities: Render inline badge span with configurable variant, size, and color
Support custom color via dynamic style props (background, border, text)
Support solid mode (opaque background, no border) and tag mode (minimal, no border)
Render children content via Svelte 5 snippet API
